TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: pai timun a nia kaka i Igiris a mapuɫat, maɫimaɫi aravats a nasekeɫang mun tua kai nua Tsemas tua kiɫivak nua Tsemas a kirimu; sa nu pasa Tayuangi anga ti Uaituun sinsi, sa ne paɫalualuayi anga tua kiɫivak nua Tsemas.
S.PHON: pai timun a nia kaka i igiris a mapuʎat, maʎimaʎi aravaʦ a nasəkəʎaŋ mun tua kai nua ʦəmas tua kiʎivak nua ʦəmas a kirimu; sa nu pasa tajuaŋi aŋa ti uaituun sinsi, sa nə paʎaɭuaɭuaji aŋa tua kiʎivak nua ʦəmas.
S.FORM: pai timun a nia kaka i Igiris a mapuljat, maljimalji aravac a nasekeljang mun tua kai nua Cemas tua kiljivak nua Cemas a kirimu; sa nu pasa Tayuangi anga ti Uaituun sinsi, sa ne paljalualuayi anga tua kiljivak nua Cemas.
S.PHON: pai timun a nia kaka i igiris a mapuʎat, maʎimaʎi aravaʦ a nasəkəʎaŋ mun tua kai nua ʦəmas tua kiʎivak nua ʦəmas a kirimu; sa nu pasa tajuaŋi aŋa ti uaituun sinsi, sa nə paʎaɭuaɭuaji aŋa tua kiʎivak nua ʦəmas.
S.TRANSL: All our brothers and sisters in England, we are very thankful that you came to know the word of God earlier, and you sent Mr Whitehorn, and the love of God was made clear to us.

S.FORM: pai ka tjaisangas ika men a kemɫang ki tjen a marekakakaka, ki matsidiɫ a tja kama a naqemati tjanuitjen a i katsauan.
S.PHON: pai ka caisaŋas ika mən a kəmʎaŋ ki cən a marəkakakaka, ki maʦidiʎ a ca kama a naqəmati canuicən a i kaʦauan.
S.FORM: pai ka tjaisangas ika men a kemljang ki tjen a marekakakaka, ki macidilj a tja kama a naqemati tjanuitjen a i kacauan.
S.PHON: pai ka ʦaisaŋas ika mən a kəmʎaŋ ki ʦən a marəkakakaka, ki maʦidiʎ a ʦa kama a naqəmati ʦanuiʦən a i kaʦauan.
S.TRANSL: Before that we did not know that we are brothers and sisters, and that we have one Father who created us all in the world.

S.FORM: ɫakua ka pasemalavan anga men ni Uaituun sinsi kaɫaluayin anga men, kemɫang anga men tu marekakakaka itjen.
S.PHON: ʎakua ka pasəmaɭavan aŋa mən ni uaituun sinsi kaʎaɭuajin aŋa mən, kəmʎaŋ aŋa mən tu marəkakakaka icən.
S.FORM: ljakua ka pasemalavan anga men ni Uaituun sinsi kaljaluayin anga men, kemljang anga men tu marekakakaka itjen.
S.PHON: ʎakua ka pasəmaɭavan aŋa mən ni uaituun sinsi kaʎaɭuajin aŋa mən, kəmʎaŋ aŋa mən tu marəkakakaka iʦən.
S.TRANSL: But when Mr Whitehorn told us we understood, and now we know that we are brothers and sisters.

S.FORM: manu marekakakaka itjen a mapuɫat a i katsauan, ayanga men.
S.PHON: manu marəkakakaka icən a mapuʎat a i kaʦauan, ajaŋa mən.
S.FORM: manu marekakakaka itjen a mapuljat a i kacauan, ayanga men.
S.PHON: manu marəkakakaka iʦən a mapuʎat a i kaʦauan, ajaŋa mən.
S.TRANSL: So now we acknowledge that all of us in the world are brothers and sisters.

S.FORM: pai a kina-i-Tayuangan ni Uaituun sinsi nuka i tsadja aravats i gadu ika puluɫaluɫayan tua pasemalaw tua kai nua Tsemas, tua tjemtjautsikel.
S.PHON: pai a kina-i-tajuaŋan ni uaituun sinsi nuka i ʦaɉa aravaʦ i gadu ika puɭuʎaɭuʎajan tua pasəmaɭaw tua kai nua ʦəmas, tua cəmcauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: pai a kina-i-Tayuangan ni Uaituun sinsi nuka i cadja aravac i gadu ika pululjaluljayan tua pasemalaw tua kai nua Cemas, tua tjemtjaucikel.
S.PHON: pai a kina-i-tajuaŋan ni uaituun sinsi nuka i ʦaɉa aravaʦ i gadu ika puɭuʎaɭuʎajan tua pasəmaɭaw tua kai nua ʦəmas, tua ʦəmʦauʦikəɭ.
S.TRANSL: During Mr Whitehorn’s time in Taiwan even in very distant places in the mountains he has not shirked telling the word of God and preaching.

S.FORM: pai kana ika pitsul nua Tsemas, kana ikana men a zinuker ni Uaituun sinsi.
S.PHON: pai kana ika piʦuɭ nua ʦəmas, kana ikana mən a zinukər ni uaituun sinsi.
S.FORM: pai kana ika picul nua Cemas, kana ikana men a zinuker ni Uaituun sinsi.
S.PHON: pai kana ika piʦuɭ nua ʦəmas, kana ikana mən a zinukər ni uaituun sinsi.
S.TRANSL: If it were not for the power of God Mr Whitehorn would not have reached us.

S.FORM: ɫakua ki anema pinapupitsulan nua Tsemas, ika napuluɫaluɫayan tu uri mapaseqeɫing amen uta, ayaya men a malevaleva; saka napaparangez anga men sakamaya tua Tsemas.
S.PHON: ʎakua ki anəma pinapupiʦuɭan nua ʦəmas, ika napuɭuʎaɭuʎajan tu uri mapasəqəʎiŋ amən uta, ajaja mən a maɭəvaɭəva; saka napaparaŋəz aŋa mən sakamaja tua ʦəmas.
S.FORM: ljakua ki anema pinapupiculan nua Cemas, ika napululjaluljayan tu uri mapaseqeljing amen uta, ayaya men a malevaleva; saka napaparangez anga men sakamaya tua Cemas.
S.PHON: ʎakua ki anəma pinapupiʦuɭan nua ʦəmas, ika napuɭuʎaɭuʎajan tu uri mapasəqəʎiŋ amən uta, ajaja mən a maɭəvaɭəva; saka napaparaŋəz aŋa mən sakamaja tua ʦəmas.
S.TRANSL: But he has been given power by God and has not shirked the task, so that we too could be saved; we gladly acknowledge this, and we have truly trusted in God.

S.FORM: pai tisun a ti Uaituun sinsi, djavadjavay aravats a su kina-i-Tayuangan.
S.PHON: pai tisun a ti uaituun sinsi, ɉavaɉavaj aravaʦ a su kina-i-tajuaŋan.
S.FORM: pai tisun a ti Uaituun sinsi, djavadjavay aravac a su kina-i-Tayuangan.
S.PHON: pai tisun a ti uaituun sinsi, ɉavaɉavaj aravaʦ a su kina-i-tajuaŋan.
S.TRANSL: Now Mr Whitehorn, we are very grateful for your time in Taiwan.

S.FORM: pai ki sun a zemuker anga itua mareka tja kaka i Igiris, ki sun a tjemautsiketsikel anga tua su kinasi-Tayuangan tua su zelian.
S.PHON: pai ki sun a zəmukər aŋa itua marəka ca kaka i igiris, ki sun a cəmauʦikəʦikəɭ aŋa tua su kinasi-tajuaŋan tua su zəɭian.
S.FORM: pai ki sun a zemuker anga itua mareka tja kaka i Igiris, ki sun a tjemaucikecikel anga tua su kinasi-Tayuangan tua su zelian.
S.PHON: pai ki sun a zəmukər aŋa itua marəka ʦa kaka i igiris, ki sun a ʦəmauʦikəʦikəɭ aŋa tua su kinasi-tajuaŋan tua su zəɭian.
S.TRANSL: Now you will return to our brothers and sisters in England, and you will be telling them of your visit to Taiwan and your labours here.

S.FORM: ika manu men a dedet a su zinuker, djavadjavay aravats, Uaituun sinsi.
S.PHON: ika manu mən a dədət a su zinukər, ɉavaɉavaj aravaʦ, uaituun sinsi.
S.FORM: ika manu men a dedet a su zinuker, djavadjavay aravac, Uaituun sinsi.
S.PHON: ika manu mən a dədət a su zinukər, ɉavaɉavaj aravaʦ, uaituun sinsi.
S.TRANSL: It is not as if we to whom you came lived near you; we are very grateful, Mr Whitehorn.

S.FORM: pai uɫa sun a kinizangan, uɫa sun a pinapupitsulan, uɫa sun a pinakatsuatsuay i katsauan nua naqemati a Tsemas tua su pasemalamalaw tua kai nua Tsemas, sa su kana tsemikel anga uta tjanuamen a i Tayuang a mareka su kaka.
S.PHON: pai uʎa sun a kinizaŋan, uʎa sun a pinapupiʦuɭan, uʎa sun a pinakaʦuaʦuaj i kaʦauan nua naqəmati a ʦəmas tua su pasəmaɭamaɭaw tua kai nua ʦəmas, sa su kana ʦəmikəɭ aŋa uta canuamən a i tajuaŋ a marəka su kaka.
S.FORM: pai ulja sun a kinizangan, ulja sun a pinapupiculan, ulja sun a pinakacuacuay i kacauan nua naqemati a Cemas tua su pasemalamalaw tua kai nua Cemas, sa su kana cemikel anga uta tjanuamen a i Tayuang a mareka su kaka.
S.PHON: pai uʎa sun a kinizaŋan, uʎa sun a pinapupiʦuɭan, uʎa sun a pinakaʦuaʦuaj i kaʦauan nua naqəmati a ʦəmas tua su pasəmaɭamaɭaw tua kai nua ʦəmas, sa su kana ʦəmikəɭ aŋa uta ʦanuamən a i tajuaŋ a marəka su kaka.
S.TRANSL: May you be guarded and strengthened, and may the creator God grant you to live long in the world for your telling of the word of God, and so may you come back again to us your brothers and sisters in Taiwan.

S.FORM: aitsu a tiamen ki men a napatjakisamuɫanga uta a ɫemaluay tua pazazekazekatj nua Tsemas, uɫa men a napenakalevanga uta tjanusun a ti, tjanusun, nu mangtjez anga sun a kitsepeliw a sema maza i Tayuang.
S.PHON: aiʦu a tiamən ki mən a napacakisamuʎaŋa uta a ʎəmaɭuaj tua pazazəkazəkac nua ʦəmas, uʎa mən a napənakaɭəvaŋa uta canusun a ti, canusun, nu maŋcəz aŋa sun a kiʦəpəɭiw a səma maza i tajuaŋ.
S.FORM: aicu a tiamen ki men a napatjakisamuljanga uta a ljemaluay tua pazazekazekatj nua Cemas, ulja men a napenakalevanga uta tjanusun a ti, tjanusun, nu mangtjez anga sun a kicepeliw a sema maza i Tayuang.
S.PHON: aiʦu a tiamən ki mən a napaʦakisamuʎaŋa uta a ʎəmaɭuaj tua pazazəkazəkaʦ nua ʦəmas, uʎa mən a napənakaɭəvaŋa uta ʦanusun a ti, ʦanusun, nu maŋʦəz aŋa sun a kiʦəpəɭiw a səma maza i tajuaŋ.
S.TRANSL: As for us we will more zealously try to understand the commands of God, so that we may gladden you, Mr-you, when you come back here to Taiwan.
